characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Abusive Parents: Her mother is very emotionally abusive towards her, being generally emotionally distant throughout Janine's childhood and ignoring any of Janine's attempts to reach out to her unless she needs something from her. Janine frequently had to be the responsible one even as a child due to her mother being incredibly irresponsible. And when Vanetta does show up to meet Janine, it's purely to emotionally manipulate her into paying for her phone bills.A lot of Janine's clinginess and people-pleasing nature stems from growing up with her mom's emotional abuse. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other:Although she and her sister Ayesha have a brief argument, they do love and care about each other.Despite her mother's emotionally neglectful ways, Janine still loves her and is willing to sacrifice her own hard-earned vacation money to help settle Vanetta's outstanding phone bill. Although she and her sister Ayesha have a brief argument, they do love and care about each other. Despite her mother's emotionally neglectful ways, Janine still loves her and is willing to sacrifice her own hard-earned vacation money to help settle Vanetta's outstanding phone bill. All-Loving Hero: Tries her hardest to see the best in everyone, including her boyfriend Tariq who most agree is wrong for her. Even Ava, who comes the closest to being the one person she starts to actively dislike, is invited to her "work family" exercise.

characters.name: Janine Teagues
characters.description: Abusive Parents: Her mother is very emotionally abusive towards her, being generally emotionally distant throughout Janine's childhood and ignoring any of Janine's attempts to reach out to her unless she needs something from her. Janine frequently had to be the responsible one even as a child due to her mother being incredibly irresponsible. And when Vanetta does show up to meet Janine, it's purely to emotionally manipulate her into paying for her phone bills.A lot of Janine's clinginess and people-pleasing nature stems from growing up with her mom's emotional abuse. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other:Although she and her sister Ayesha have a brief argument, they do love and care about each other.Despite her mother's emotionally neglectful ways, Janine still loves her and is willing to sacrifice her own hard-earned vacation money to help settle Vanetta's outstanding phone bill. Although she and her sister Ayesha have a brief argument, they do love and care about each other. Despite her mother's emotionally neglectful ways, Janine still loves her and is willing to sacrifice her own hard-earned vacation money to help settle Vanetta's outstanding phone bill. All-Loving Hero: Tries her hardest to see the best in everyone, including her boyfriend Tariq who most agree is wrong for her. Even Ava, who comes the closest to being the one person she starts to actively dislike, is invited to her "work family" exercise.

characters.name: Barbara Howard
characters.description: Age Insecurity: Barbara overall hates anything that suggests that she is getting old, with it being one of her few weak spots (as she will do outright foolish things to deny it). In “Zoo Balloon”, she overreacts to discovering the Zoo has retired her favourite lizard, clearly projecting her own fears of being randomly forced out of a job she loves. In “100 Days of School”, she has a near breakdown upon discovering the students all think she’s a lot older than she actually is (to the point her students honestly believe she’s 100 years old, and even an older one assume she’s in her sixties). Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: While she's often annoyed by Janine, she shows that she does care abut her time and time again, and will drop everything to comfort her if she's upset. Brutally Honest: Barbara doesn't mince words, as shown in the pilot.Ava:[about Barbara never complaining or asking for things]What is your secret, Barb?Barbara:Knowing there's not much you can do, Ava. Church Lady:Deeplyreligious and involved in her church. Sometimes this results in hilarious plots where she is involved in some intrigue among the church ladies of her congregation; other times, it makes her a resource for Abbott who can rally her church to help the school and students when they are in a jam. Cool Old Lady: Her age hasn't slowed her down one bit.

characters.name: Melissa Schemmenti
characters.description: Actor Allusion: Scenes of her at home are heavily influenced by Lisa Ann Walter's role inThe Parent Trap (1998), from the passion for cooking down to the outfit she's wearing. Age Insecurity: Refuses to state her age, even calling herself a "divorced, none-of-your-business year-old woman," to the camera crew. Ambiguously Bi: Describes herself as a "mostly straight woman". Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Despite their bitter relationship, Melissa and her sister Kristin still care deeply about each other. Batter Up!: She keeps a baseball bat duct-taped underneath her desk for emergencies and has another one at home that she sleeps with.

characters.name: Gregory Eddie
characters.description: Aesop Amnesia: Gregory has to be reminded to lighten up regularly. Even after the Season 1 finale where the lesson finally seems to stick, he still reverts to what he knows when faced with a new challenge. Almighty Janitor: As a long-term sub, Gregory isn't even a full employee of Abbott Elementary. However, he was initially hired as the school's principal for averygood reason. When he has to help Ava do her job to secure funding for the school, he manages to do more in a single afternoon than she has done all year. Aside Glance: A MASTER at these, especially given theMockumentaryformat of the show. Audience Surrogate: Serves as the "new guy" and we see some of the worst of Abbott through his eyes. Caring Gardener: Due to being forced to work for his family business through his entire childhood, Gregory is an extremely knowledgeable gardener, but absolutelyhatesgardening. Even so, he's more than willing to secretly tend to Barbara and Jacob's garden when it's clear that they have no idea what they're doing.

characters.name: Jacob Hill
characters.description: Abusive Parents: It's heavily implied that he has these. He talks about not enjoying Christmas in "Holiday Hookah" and has a very negative reaction (unusual for Jacob, who's usuallyThe Pollyanna) to seeing his brother at Abbott because he associates him with their parents. All Gays Love Theatre: He's practically shouting when he suggests a field trip to see a Broadway musical. Birds of a Feather: He and Janine are both newer, younger teachers who have very similar enthusiastic and upbeat personalities especially when it comes to teaching and they both have intentions to make school a better experience for kids. Naturally, they get along very well and humorously enough they tend to annoy the older staff. The Bore: Downplayed in that he's actually very upbeat and energetic, but still manages to go on tangents the other teachers have no interest in. A frequent gag is for Jacob to reference something or start telling a story only for another character to cut him off, ignoring him. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: He's often awkward, naive, and overbearing, but Jacob is a good teacher and his students do like his class. Ava even ends up extending an audit of his class during Black History Month just so she can learn more from him.As gross as his "carrot cookies" seem, when Melissa actually tries one, she admits it's delicious.

characters.name: Ava Coleman
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: Played with. She's very into Greg and he is very NOT interested but despite her continued flirtations, she hasn't gone totally overboard...yet. Alpha Bitch: Routinely belittles and bullies her staff, especially Janine. Ambiguously Bi:She says all the faculty members all make good eye candy, and not just the guys, and goes on to gush about Barbara, Melissa, and "Janine, on a good day."She says that Melissa has a "bangin' body."She goes on onscreen dates with multiple men.She calls herself "a top" in aDouble Entendreabout pyramid schemes.From time to time, she also references gay anthems such asGood Luck, Babe!She uses gender-neutral language such as "people" and "professional athletes" when describing her long list of exes.Pretty much unambiguous in the Valentine's Day episode, in which she claims to be "the one that got away" for "athletes, oligarchs, thespians, and lesbians." She says all the faculty members all make good eye candy, and not just the guys, and goes on to gush about Barbara, Melissa, and "Janine, on a good day." She says that Melissa has a "bangin' body."

characters.name: Mr. Johnson
characters.description: Almighty Janitor: In addition to being a literal janitor, he's almost singlehandedly responsible for keeping the Abbott building running, and the situation can get very bad if he's not around to fix things. He's also led a hell of a life and seems almost supernaturally lucky, such as when hewins the basketball tickets AND the courtside trivia contest in "Teacher Appreciation." Ambiguously Bi: If you can even call it "ambiguous." He visits a female strip club, and also openly ogles O'Shon and addresses him as "handsome." Amusing Third-Party Interjection: A solid third of Mr Johnson's lines are him chiming into other people's conversations with wacky comments. Berserk Button: Becomes very angry that Greg doesn't like pizza. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Calling him "eccentric" would be an understatement, and he does tend to slack off when he can get away with it. However, he's a supernaturally talented custodian who is keeping Abbott running almost single-handedly.

characters.name: Tariq Temple
characters.description: Janine's longterm boyfriend at the start of Season 1, whom she has long since outgrown. Though they break up at the end of Season 1, circumstances continue to keep Tariq in the school's orbit. Alliterative Name:TariqTemple. Amicable Exes: He and Janine aren't exactly friends after the breakup, but there's no animosity and she's more mildly exasperated by him than angered. She's sincerely glad he's getting his act together. Childhood Friend Romance: He and Janine have been together since they were in middle school.This is not a good thing; the two are onverydifferent tracks in life and have mutually incompatible personalities. They're only still together out of inertia, which Janine only realizes when she admits this to her coworkers. Drugs Are Bad: Janine gets him a job with F.A.D.E. — a parody of the D.A.R.E. drug prevention program — which employs him to inflict forced, heavy-handed rap lyrics about drugs on children.

characters.name: Zach
characters.description: Jacob's boyfriend and a shoe aficionado. Amicable Exes: In the Season 3 finale,Janine manages to convince him and Jacob to talk with each other which leads to them making up and becoming good friends. Beautiful Singing Voice: As shown in the first season finale when he riffs off "The Wheels on the Bus" as a chaperone on the school field trip. Berserk Button: Jacob mentions that leaving without saying goodbyeinfuriates him. Beware the Nice Ones:After finding out Jacob was purposefully doing petty actions in order to anger him to get him to break up with him first so he wouldn’t have to do it, Zach is appalled and warns Jacob that he "unleashed the kraken", and he retaliates by shrinking his clothes and forcing him to find a new apartment.

characters.name: Kristin Marie
characters.description: Melissa's estranged sister who works at a rival school. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Despite their bitter relationship, Kristin and her sister Melissa still care deeply about each other. Enemy Mine: She and Melissa briefly team up to intimidate a passing teacher who makes a cheap shot at Kirsten's fashion choices. Once Done, Never Forgotten: Melissa's grudge towards her sister stems from how sheabandonedher dying grandmother leaving Melissa to be the one to deal with her all alone. Pet the Dog: After having made amends with Melissa, She visits her at Abbott to inform her that Legendary Charter School is coming for Abbott.

characters.name: Taylor Howard
characters.description: Surprise Pregnancy: In Season 4, she reveals to her mother, Barbara, that she’s pregnant with a girl. Temporary Love Interest: She dates Gregory, but it doesn't last long due to there beingNo Sparks. "Well Done, Son" Guy: She wants her mom to be proud of her, and is frustrated that she doesn't seem to respect her life choices.

characters.name: Martin Eddie
characters.description: Gregory's father, a strict militaristic gardener and the reason he's the man he is today. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: In "Fight" Martin shared that he was planning on renaming Eddie Lawn and Care to Eddie and Son Lawn Care. He told Gregory that he did not want his son working for him, but rather that he worked with him. He was hard on him because he wanted to know that Gregory could carry his own weight. Dad the Veteran: He brings his ex-army military style to how he runs his business and treated young Gregory like a recruit. Hidden Depths: In "Please Touch Museum", he shows a secret playful side as he goofs around with Janine and the 2nd graders during their field trip. He explains to his son that he's always had it but had to cover it up after his wife passed away. Like Parent, Like Child: In the Season 4 finale,he confesses to Greggo that he also fell in love with a playful, free-spirited woman which helped him express his silly side. Just like how Gregory is able to show his playfulness thanks to Janine.

characters.name: Ayesha Teagues
characters.description: Janine's estranged sister who lives in Denver, Colorado. She comes to Philadelphia to see Janine in Season 2. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Although she and Janine do have a brief argument with each other, they do love and care about each other. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: She is the foolish to Janine's responsible and educated. It is deconstructed as Ayesha is very bothered because everyone gave up on her making something of herself because she isn't like Janine, while for Janine, it always left her to pick up the pieces.

characters.name: Vanetta Teagues
characters.description: Janine and Ayesha's mother. A selfish, materialistic woman who is a huge source for Janine's personal problems. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Despite her neglectful relationship with Janine, she does love her daughter and wants her to be happy. Unfortunately, she is also extremely cynical and self-centered, and every time her relationship with Janine seems to be improving, Vanetta's own ego will cause it to tank. Green-Eyed Monster: While Vanetta claims she has Janine’s interests at heart, her first instinct when hearing about Janine’s happiness with Gregory or plans for the future on her 30th birthday is to shoot her down or to project insecurities onto her, with Barbara figuring that she’s jealous of her daughter having a fulfilling and successful adulthood in ways she didn’t. Mama Bear: She makes it clear to Ava during their first meeting that regardless of their estrangement, she won't tolerate her daughter being insulted.Ava:Damn, genetics are wild! You're cool and she—[Vanetta gives her a threatening look]Ava (scared):—you would beat my ass, wouldn't you?"Vanetta:Hm.[Ava runs away]Ava:(during a talking head segment)The key to never getting your ass beat? Knowing when someone can beat your ass. Honor Thy Abuser: Vanetta is extremely neglectful but she does stand up to Ava for Janine and makes it clear that she'll stick up for her daughter.

characters.name: Caleb Hill
characters.description: All American Boy: The reason why their parents prefer Caleb is because he was the athletic, popular, heteronormative child they always wanted. Annoying Younger Sibling: Played with. Jacob is irritated by him, but it turns outthey really do love each other. Big Brother Instinct: Comes very close to punching Mellisa’s elderlyuncle Archiein “Winter Break” for making a homophobic remark about Jacob, to the point Jacob has to restrain him. Big Brother Worship: Admits that he always looked up to Jacob and was impressed by the fact that he never compromised on who he was, in spite of all the forces that attempted to suppress and change him throughout his life, in particular, their parents. Brainless Beauty: Caleb is a personal trainer, but he's pretty dim from what he says.

characters.name: Frank Coleman
characters.description: Ava's estranged father who works as a barber in Philadelphia. He is introduced when he happens to cut Gregory’s hair, and is brought onto the science fair as a sponsor, hoping to get close to Ava. Disowned Parent: Ava refuses to let Frank back into her life. Along with becoming serious and stern, she calls him by his first name and keeps her interactions with him short and to the point. Must Make Amends: He acknowledges that him abandoning Ava and her mother was unforgivable. So he wants to own up to his mistakes by offering to be more involved in her present life if she wants him to. Parental Abandonment: One of many reasons why Ava detests her father is because she left her to start a new family. Pet the Dog: He and his community helps his daughter and the Abbott staff in defense when The District Supervisors suspend them.

characters.name: Shanae and Devin
characters.description: The school's cafeteria workers. Hidden Depths: Shanae is a fan of Afrofuturist literature, effortlessly schooling the teachers on their misreading ofParable of the Sower.

characters.name: Amber
characters.description: The parent of a student in Gregory, later Janine's class who brings her son into school late. She also starts dating Gregory after her son is no longer a student in his class. It's All About Me: In her first episode, she keeps bringing her son to class late because it's more convenient for her work schedule. Downplayed, as once Gregory explains the consequences of this, she quickly corrects it. Romantic False Lead: To Gregory.

characters.name: Mr. Morton
characters.description: An eighth-grade science teacher who works with Jacob. Butt-Monkey: His ongoing acrimonious divorce in Season 3 makes him this, with constant reference to his disastrous personal life and the other teachers being unsympathetic. Enemy Mine: Downplayed. He and Jacob temporarily team up to explain to Melissa how tough it is to take care of middle schoolers while bringing up extreme examples from their exhausting experiences. The Friend Nobody Likes: Whilst he's initially included in the teachers' book club, he quickly alienates them (they even pair him with Barbara because she'll tolerate him out of Christian charity). By the end of the season pretty much all the teachers like him just as much as Jacob. Flanderization: Starts off as a somewhat humorless and straight-edged science teacher who only Jacob has an issue with. By Season 3 he's a bitterJerkasswho alternates between lamenting about his divorce and spouting sexist lines.

characters.name: Ashley Garcia
characters.description: The teaching aide assigned to assist Melissa in Season 2. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: She's easily bored, goofs around with a classroom skeleton when she should be helping Melissa, and somehow didn't see a bunch of children steal sackfuls of candy that wereright behind her. Big Fun: She's plus-sized and very cheerful and fun-oriented. The students love how she entertains them in class; too bad this is to the detriment of Melissa's actual teaching. Bumbling Sidekick: Melissadefinitelysees her as one. Ashley loves teaching and gets along well with the students, but she is clearly uninterested in anything Melissa specifically asks her to do and never consults with Melissa about lesson plans. Odd Friendship: She starts to have on with Janine near the end of "Aide" despite her ditziness.

characters.name: Krystal
characters.description: The mother of a student in Barbara's class who wears inappropriate clothing. Big Beautiful Woman: She's a heavy-set woman and is very attractive. Good Parents: She's regarded as one of Abbott's most dedicated parents as she's very attentive towards her daughter's education. Hidden Depths: A large part of her introductory episode. Barbara at first thinks that Krystal is an inattentive single mother who needs some help getting her life on track. Krystal is actually a happily married business owner with a strong brand ethos, who's deeply concerned about local issues and the vice-president of the PTA—she simply has herInnocently Insensitivemoments. Innocently Insensitive: Her episode debut has her coming to Abbott, an elementary school environment, with the "B word" tattoo exposed for everyone to see. She even has some outfits that are just as profane. Krystal admits that she is rather busy and sometimes forget to read the room.

characters.name: Erika
characters.description: A friend of Janine's who is the aunt of a student. The Gadfly: She likes to poke fun at Greggo to make him squirm, especially when it comes to his budding relationship with Janine. Gossipy Hens: She likes to shoot the breeze with Janine over any chisme she has to offer. Nice Girl: From what we see, she is the only real friend (outside Abbott) that doesn't have ulterior motives for their friendship with Janine. She is also a huge factor in teaching Janine how to have a more social life. Old Friend: Was one of the few friends Janine had in high school.

characters.name: Manny
characters.description: A new employee of the school district who convinces Janine to work with them. Acquainted in Real Life: He and Janine were previously frequently interacted on educational websites' comment sections before meeting at Abbott. All Love Is Unrequited: He's set up as another suitor for Janine and is genuinely interested in getting closer with her.Alas, Janine doesn't feel the same way and never pursues him. Commonality Connection: He mentions to Mrs. Howard how his mother was also an experienced teacher who was vocally disappointed with how her school district failed her. This revelation manages to make a dent in Barbara's defenses. Good Is Not Soft: Despite being a happy, chipper, pleasant, sunshine-babydoll,he's the one who ends Ava's principal job after she's caught taking unauthorized bribes from the golf course.

characters.name: Emily and Simon
characters.description: Employees of the School District of Philadelphia that work alongside Manny and (later) Janine. Birds of a Feather: They instantly get along with Janine thanks to sharing the same chipper, carefree and friendly nature as she does. Power Trio: The two are often shown alongside Manny. Straight Gay: The Season 3 finale reveals that Emily is a lesbian. Those Two Guys: They are usually seen together, being the only other School District employees with any focus.

characters.name: Rosalyn Inez
characters.description: A librarian hired by the district for Abbott, being the first school to take part in a library program organized by Janine. Actor Allusion: Befitting a librarian, Miss Inez likes to give colorful voices to the characters she reads about which the kids adore. Cree Summer is a legendary voice actress known for having hundreds of voices that's entertained children for decades. Friend to All Children: She is very friendly with the students and even reads them their favorite books. Hidden Depths: She's part witch as she knows how to read tarot cards. Nice Girl: She is very kind and helpful the others with their books they want to read.

characters.name: Dia
characters.description: An assistant principal of Abbott Elementary who has to put up with Ava's nonsense. Deadpan Snarker: She makes alot of dry and witty remarks when it comes to her coworkers shenanigans. The Dog Was the Mastermind: Dia of all people turns out to be the mole helping the students. Everyone Has Standards: Dia, despite having pranked the staff crew throughout the entire episode, is shocked and concerned when Jacob revealed that Morton is in the hospital. Freudian Excuse: Turns out one of the reasons Dia pranked the staff was because no one never remembered or celebrated her birthday.

characters.name: O’Shon
characters.description: O'Shon is an IT representative from the district who goes behind their back to protect Abbott after they begin taking unauthorized equipment from the nearby golf course construction crew. He forms a connection with Ava, one that is at-first one-sided. Incredibly Lame Fun: As a kid, he dreamed of secretly living in a Circuit City. Nice Guy: O'Shon is a genuinely pleasant and kind man, who is also extremely patient with Abbott shenanigans. Savvy Guy, Energetic Girl: He becomes the grounded and sensible savvy guy to Ava's over-the-top and ambitious energetic girl. Ship Tease: It's evident from his first episode that there is some mutual attraction between him and Ava, although Ava is determined to play hard-to-get.

characters.name: Dominic Clark
characters.description: Friendship Denial: Dominic confirms that he doesn't view Morton as a friend since he only came for a free drink (which was really just water). Logical Latecomer: As a newbie introduced in Season 5, Dominic is observant of the shenanigans and power structure that goes on in Abbott. Particularly with how the only ones who get to do fun or interesting things are Principal Ava and her inner circle. Stern Teacher: He eventually gets his class to respect/obey his authority by threatening to destroy a cherished crane machine.

characters.name: Denzel Collins
characters.description: The Philadelphia school district's original superintendent that Ava blackmailed to get her job. Asshole Victim: He's blackmailed by Ava into giving her a principal job. It'd be easier to feel sorry for him if he didn't cheat on his wife. Kick the Dog: While he has every reason to hate Ava (shedidblackmail him), it's just plain petty of him to take it out on Abbott Elementary by threatening to cut their funding. While this would certainly hurt Ava,it would also hurt the completely innocent teachers and students, as Ava herself points out. Put on a Bus: Something happened where he lost his superintendent position to John Reynolds in Season 3 and is no longer a prominent character. Remarried to the Mistress: He reveals to Ava that he decided to dump his old wife and marry his mistress, meaning that Ava has no power over him and he can get rid of her whenever he wants.

characters.name: Draemond Winding
characters.description: The owner of Legendary Schools, a charter organization that comes into conflict with Abbott after releasing an attack ad about the school's teachers. Affably Evil: To the point, he doesn't do anything illegal when he makes an ad portraying Abbott Elementary School in a negative light (even if he did trick them into signing contracts allowing him to do it). Even when confronted about it, he compliments Jacob by comparing him toTimothée Chalamet, tries to convince the teachers that he was only helping them, and still has a cordial talk with Barbara. Of course, even after he agrees to have the ad pulled, he privately decides that he's now planning on turning Abbott into one of his charter schools - but he still insists it's to help the kids. Arc Villain: He's the main antagonist of Season 2'sLegendary CharterArc. Freudian Excuse: He came from a severely underprivileged background to the point where he was jaded and cynical at the age offive. Aside from Barbara’s class, he had a horrible time in public schools until he was enrolled in a charter school he thrived in. This resulted in starting his own charter organization with the goal of converting every elementary school in Philadelphia to one. Jerkass Has a Point: As reprehensible as his actions are, he isn't wrong that Abbott has a lot of problems as an institution of learning, particularly the fact that Ava is clearly unqualified to be a principal.

characters.name: Crystal
characters.description: A former sorority sister of Ava who became the principal of Liberty Rings Elementary from the same school district. Her school often comes into clash with the teachers of Abbott, specifically with Ava. Beneath the Mask: Crystal, who's normally shown to be a cool and suave woman, is emotionally distressed over being rejected by the District Despots. She then vents to her henchman on how she won't have the heart to face the kids and tell them that she wasn't able to get them better resources like they promised. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: She puts on a friendly and cheerful persona if only to fool others into letting their guard down. Enemy Mine: Despite her differences with each Ava,Crystal defends her in "Rally" from the District Despots despite having been the one who ratted the latter out in the first place. Malicious Misnaming: Purposefully calls Ava "soror" just to get under her skin.

characters.name: Miles Nathaniel
characters.description: A PGA lawyer who is blackmailed by Abbott when they learn that the golf course is utilizing non-unionized workers. After this, they continue to find way: to blackmail Nathaniel, with him eventually coming back and using them for clout. Amoral Attorney: He shows up to Abbott to sway the teachers with bribes and free computers. He also makes it clear that the construction of the golf course will still happen regardless if they accept his bribes or not. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Though he appears to be a professional person, this is mostly a facade to hide his true nature. Card-Carrying Villain: While he can put up aFaux Affably Evilfacade if needed, behind closed doors he doesn't make any effort to hide his corrupt nature, blatantly describing his actions as bribery and blackmail. At one point he drops even the facade and walks into a meeting to brag about how he's bribed everyone in the city. Can't Kill You, Still Need You: Mr. Nathaniel mentions that the three-legged dog should have been executed for trespassing on the golf course per the course's laws. But he decided to spare the poor puppy by exploiting him for his publicity stunt.

characters.name: Superintendent Audits
characters.description: The Supervisors of the school district. Evil Is Petty: After Melissa mentions that she has a dependent on school grounds who relies on her (Sweet Cheeks), one of the Despots decides to suspend Sweet Cheeks alongside her. Pet the Dog:In “Audits”, They let the teachers pursue their jobs after they were exposed for having technology at their school.In “Rally”, the School Audits let the teachers have their job back, same for Ava having her job as principal again. In “Audits”, They let the teachers pursue their jobs after they were exposed for having technology at their school. In “Rally”, the School Audits let the teachers have their job back, same for Ava having her job as principal again.

characters.name: Tina Schwartz
characters.description: The former teacher at Abbott Elementary who was fired for kicking a child. She was later hired to work at Addington Charter School. The Bus Came Back: She turned out to have been hired at a charter school. She later returns in season 5 as a candidate for a minor political office that she is completely unqualified for, inspiring Jacob to run against her. Karma Houdini: She was fired from Abbott for kicking a student, but then got a job at the more prestigious and better-paying Addington, since it's a charter school and they don't have as many restrictions on who can be hired. Season 5 reveals that her firing does not prevent her from running for a minor politicial office. Put on a Bus: She was fired after kicking a child out of frustration. Small Role, Big Impact: Although Tina was fired in the first episode and rarely appeared afterward, her firing led to her successor, Gregory Eddie, coming in as a substitute teacher for her class and becoming a major cast member. Not to mention that she is indirectly responsible for theShip Teasebetween him and Janine to happen. Had she not "punted" a student, Gregory would not have been hired to take her place and met Janine.

characters.name: Delisha Sloss
characters.description: Delisha Sloss is a member of the Philadelphia School Board member and church finance committee chair whose petty antagonism and corrupt dealings position her as a smug adversary to Abbott Elementary's underfunded staff. She targets Barbara Howard with pious barbs across their shared congregation, flipping moral leverage in funding disputes. Asshole Victim: Sister Slosh ends up on the receiving end of a slap during a PTA meeting. Blackmail: Using her connections, from the meeting, Melissa figures out that, Delisha Sloss, has been extorting money from the local church and wants to use that information to hers and Barbara's advantage. And after reconsidering her priorities regarding her morals and friendship, Barbara decides to do her part in blackmailing Sloss to get fund money. Hypocrite: Delisha preaches churchly morals and fiscal piety while skimming from the finance committee for years, yet condemns others' ethical shortcuts—especially Barbara Howard's—to mask her own graftShe haggles Abbott kids' candy at $1 (after Ava's $2 scam), then sneers at Barbara for "shaking down people once again," as if her board neglect and church thefts never forced suchShe stonewalls Abbott's funding pleas with smug restraint lectures, ignoring her embezzlement history that mirrors the very shortfalls she helps perpetuate. She haggles Abbott kids' candy at $1 (after Ava's $2 scam), then sneers at Barbara for "shaking down people once again," as if her board neglect and church thefts never forced such

characters.name: Maurice
characters.description: A friend of Gregory who begins dating Janine midway through Season 2. Temporary Love Interest: He briefly dates Janine but they end things due to not being compatible and her having feelings for his friend.

characters.name: Cassidy Geoffrey
characters.description: A new teacher taking Janine’s place in her former classroom. Asian Airhead: They are of Asian descent and have a rather scatterbrained personality, leading Melissa to doubt their teaching qualifications. It doesn't help that they incorporate some bizarre classroom management skills, like having the kids scream their hearts out as a brain break. Friend to All Children: They are pretty good and friendly to the students in Janine’s class. Irony: They were introduced as a bizarre substitute who used weird and unconventional teaching methods. Yet they consider Abbott teachers to be too weird even for their standards. Nice Guy: They are very nice and helpful, including towards Melissa.

characters.name: Elizabeth Washington
characters.description: A representative from the local news station who visits Abbott to document an award ceremony. Exact Words: The Blonde Despot assures the kids at Abbott that they'll hold a special council where they'll listen to their concerns about their teachers being suspended. Doesn't mean that they're actually going to give them their jobs back. Pet the Dog: In “Rally”, the School Audits let the teachers have their job pack same for Ava having her job as principal again. Womanchild: She appears to having a temper of a child in "Rally" as she threw her lunch at the glass ordering the teachers to come back to the meeting.

characters.name: Sister Sipp
characters.description: Cool Old Lady: She warmly affirms Barbara's vision ("Right you are, Sister Howard") before pivoting; handles Ava's outbursts indirectly through process. Genre Savvy: She recognizes "Shackles" needing Sheila's style, dodging Barbara's power play without confrontation. Reasonable Authority Figure: She allows unorthodox auditions like Barbara's "O Happy Day" but sticks to Sheila's key for the Triumphant Love Concert, prioritizing harmony over favoritism.

characters.name: Willy R. Abbott V
characters.description: A direct descendent of school founder Willard R. Abbott.

characters.name: Timothy
characters.description: A F.A.D.E. performer who aspires to be like Tariq.

characters.name: Caroline
characters.description: An unhelpful F.A.D.E. representative whose speech discouraging drug use quickly goes off the rails.

characters.name: John Reynolds
characters.description: The district's new superintendent who approves of Janine's library program. He is adamant about the district spending as little money as possible, and when he offers Janine a full-time job, she rejects it, wanting to return to teaching instead of helping every school in the district. Disproportionate Retribution: Even though Superintendent Reynolds accepts Janine's resignation, he does bring up that he will be holding a grudge against Abbott for as long as his heart is pumping. Kick the Dog: As soon as the Superintendent learns that Janine's new library program will require funds, he shuts it down and tosses the folder into the trash.

characters.name: Warren
characters.description: Janine's coworker during her stint at the district, working for Human Resources. He is often openly expressive about how much he dislikes Janine, and is not affected when she leaves. Effeminate Voice: He has a manner of speaking full of feminine lilt and overall snootiness. Jerkass to One: During her time working at the district, Warren would always give Janine a hard time or brushed her off. Later, he proudly admits that he hated Janine and is glad that she's gone. Reasonable Authority Figure: Putting aside his hatred for Janine, Warren is very professional when it comes to his job and takes his district cases both seriously and fairly. In fact,he gives the okay for Janine and Gregory's relationship since they're both mature adults with no power imbalance between them. Sissy Villain: A feminine man who despises the show's kindhearted heroine.

characters.name: Avi
characters.description: Avi is an EMT who works alongside Captain Robinson and later begins hanging out with Jacob, however, they are not romantically involved. He introduces his friend Olivia to Gregory during a double date, however Gregory's attraction to Janine causes the duo to not work out.

characters.name: Olivia
characters.description: Olivia is an EMT and a coworker of Avi who is invited on a double date with him, Jacob and Gregory. However, the sudden appearance of Janine and Manny at the bar the group goes to, causes friction in the date. Nice Girl: Olivia is quite chipper, friendly and patient, especially considering that Gregory is not the best date during their double date. Screw This, I'm Outta Here!: Olivia is fairly patient but there's only so long that she can put up with the fact that Gregory only has eyes for Janine and leaves before the end of the date.

characters.name: The Volunteers
characters.description: A group of familiar but unsavory characters who are assigned a week's worth of community service at Abbott. For more about them, seehere.

characters.name: Tammy
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: Tammy delivers every verdict and comeback with the same flat, unhurried energy. When Mr. Johnson mishears "and/or" as the Star Wars seriesAndor, she doesn't miss a beat, "So you're unprepared and uncultured." Noodle Incident: Whatever really happened with the clogged DMV toilet is never elaborated on, but Tammy's immediate reaction when Mr. Ronson threatens to expose her makes it very clear it was bad enough to hold over her head. Obstructive Bureaucrat: Every decision she makes is technically correct and fully justified. Blue ink instead of black? Start over. Expired license and you drove here? Hearing test too. She isn't cruel, she's just rigidly, immovably right. Pet the Dog: When Mr. Ronson intervenes on Mr. Johnson's behalf, Tammy not only approves the REAL ID but personally directs him to Jeff and handles it cleanly, suggesting she respects loyalty and history between people even if she won't show it to strangers. Sour Outside, Sad Inside: In spite of her frigid and heartless appearance, Tammy is shown to also dislike her job at the DMV because of how dark and soul-crushing it is. She isn't even phased when Melissa swears to see her in hell because she's already there.

characters.name: Jeff
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: When Ava demands he retake her photo and count down properly, he simply says "Three" and snaps it anyway, completely unbothered by her frustration. Pet the Dog: At the end of the episode he gives Melissa a full proper countdown and even compliments her photo, calling it "stunning."

characters.name: Mr. Ronson
characters.description: Almighty Janitor: Despite being the lowliest employee in the building, Mr. Ronson turns out to be the most important person in the entire DMV visit — the only one with enough leverage and history to actually solve Mr. Johnson's problem. "Blackmail" Is Such an Ugly Word: When Tammy refuses to help, Mr. Ronson calmly threatens to tell everyone she was responsible for clogging the DMV toilet, and it works instantly. Old Friend: He is an old friend of Mr. Johnson's who helped him out when he was in a dark place (working in a Sears basement). He appears asOne-Scene Wondernear the end to help Mr. Johnson out of his predicament.
